name: Robert McGregor
metaTitle: Robert McGregor Paintings for Sale: Bedford Fine Art Gallery
metaDescription: Explore original oil paintings by renowned Scottish artist Robert McGregor (1847–1922). View our curated collection for sale at Bedford Fine Art Gallery.
body:
Robert McGregor (1847–1922) was a highly celebrated Scottish painter best known for his poignant, realistic portrayals of rural and coastal working-class life.<!----> Although born in Yorkshire, England, McGregor spent his formative years in Scotland, initially working as a book illustrator in Edinburgh before formally training at the Royal Scottish Academy Life Schools.<!----> He became one of the first Scottish artists to fully dedicate himself to rural genre painting, finding his greatest inspiration in the daily routines of ordinary people. Traveling extensively across Scotland, coastal France (particularly Brittany), and the Netherlands, McGregor masterfully captured the quiet dignity of fishermen, shepherds, farm laborers, and crofters.

Stylistically, McGregor’s work bridged native Scottish traditions with the powerful influences of French Realism and the Dutch Hague School.<!----> Deeply inspired by artists like Jean-François Millet and Anton Mauve, McGregor developed a sophisticated tonal approach characterized by a muted, earthy palette and subtle gradations of natural light. Rather than romanticizing the harsh realities of agricultural and maritime labor, his compositions often pushed figures to the foreground, creating an intimate, grounded connection between the subject and the viewer. Elected as a full Academician of the Royal Scottish Academy in 1889, McGregor remains a pivotal figure in the development of 19th-century British genre painting, leaving behind a legacy of authentic, atmospheric works that continue to captivate collectors today.
